Implementation, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–84, div. A, title V, § 541(d)123 Stat. 2298Oct. 28, 2009“(1).—PriorityA priority of the program required by , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a), to resolve missing person cases arising before the enactment of chapter 76 of such title by section 569 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1996 (; ) [approved ] shall be the return of missing persons to United States control alive.section 1509 of title 10Public Law 104–106110 Stat. 336Feb. 10, 1996“(2).—Accounting for goalIn implementing the program, the Secretary of Defense, in coordination with the officials specified in subsection (f)(1) of , United States Code, shall provide such funds, personnel, and resources as the Secretary considers appropriate to increase significantly the capability and capacity of the Department of Defense, the Armed Forces, and commanders of the combatant commands to account for missing persons so that, beginning with fiscal year 2015, the POW/MIA accounting community has sufficient resources to ensure that at least 200 missing persons are accounted for under the program annually.section 1509 of title 10“(3).—DefinitionsIn this subsection:“(A)The term ‘accounted for’ has the meaning given such term in , United States Code.section 1513(3)(B) of title 10“(B)The term ‘POW/MIA accounting community’ has the meaning given such term in section 1509(b)(2) of such title.”

children.text: World War II during the period beginning on , and ending on , including members of the armed forces who were lost during flight operations in the Pacific theater of operations covered by section 576 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2000 (; note).December 7, 1941December 31, 1946Public Law 106–6510 U.S.C. 1501
The Cold War during the period beginning on , and ending on .September 2, 1945August 21, 1991
The Korean War during the period beginning on , and ending on .June 27, 1950January 31, 1955
The Indochina War era during the period beginning on , and ending on .July 8, 1959May 15, 1975
The Persian Gulf War during the period beginning on , and ending on .August 2, 1990February 28, 1991
Such other conflicts in which members of the armed forces served as the Secretary of Defense may designate.
The Secretary of Defense shall implement the program within the Department of Defense through the designated Agency Director.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary shall assign or detail to the designated Defense Agency on a full-time basis a senior medical examiner from the personnel of the Armed Forces Medical Examiner System. The primary duties of the medical examiner so assigned or detailed shall include the identification of remains in support of the function of the designated Agency Director to account for unaccounted for persons covered by subsection (a).

children.label: B
children.text: In carrying out functions under this chapter, the medical examiner so assigned or detailed shall report to the designated Agency Director.

children.label: i
children.text: exercise scientific identification authority;

children.label: ii
children.text: establish identification and laboratory policy consistent with the Armed Forces Medical Examiner System; and

children.label: iii
children.text: advise the designated Agency Director on forensic science disciplines.

children.label: D
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be interpreted as affecting the authority of the Armed Forces Medical Examiner under .section 1471 of this title

children.heading: .—Treatment as Missing Persons
children.label: c .—Treatment as Missing Persons
children.text: Each unaccounted for person covered by subsection (a) shall be considered to be a missing person for purposes of the applicability of other provisions of this chapter to the person.

children.heading: .—Establishment of Personnel Files; Centralized Database
children.label: d .—Establishment of Personnel Files; Centralized Database
A
children.text: possesses any information relevant to the status of the person; or

children.label: B
children.text: receives any new information regarding the missing person as provided in subsection (e).

The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that each file established under this subsection contains all relevant information pertaining to a person covered by subsection (a) and is readily accessible to all elements of the department, the combatant commands, and the armed forces involved in the effort to account for the person.

Each file established under this subsection shall be handled in accordance with, and subject to the provisions of, in the same manner as applies to the file of a missing person otherwise subject to such section.section 1506 of this title

The Secretary of Defense shall establish and maintain a single centralized database and case management system containing information on all missing persons for whom a file has been established under this subsection. The database and case management system shall be accessible to all elements of the Department of Defense involved in the search, recovery, identification, and communications phases of the program established by this section.

If new information (as described in paragraph (3)) is found or received that may be related to one or more unaccounted for persons covered by subsection (a), whether or not such information specifically relates (or may specifically relate) to any particular such unaccounted for person, that information shall be provided to the Secretary of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: the information is treated under paragraph (2) of subsection (c) of , relating to addition of the information to the personnel file of a person and notification requirements, in the same manner as information received under paragraph (1) under such subsection; andsection 1505 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the information is treated under paragraph (3) of subsection (c) and subsection (d) of such section, relating to a board review under such section, in the same manner as information received under paragraph (1) of such subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: is found or received after , by a United States intelligence agency, by a Department of Defense agency, or by a person specified in ; orNovember 18, 1997section 1504(g) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is identified after , in records of the United States as information that could be relevant to the case of one or more unaccounted for persons covered by subsection (a).November 18, 1997

In carrying out the program, the designated Agency Director shall ensure coordination with the Secretaries of the military departments, the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and the commanders of the combatant commands.

In carrying out the program, the Secretary of Defense shall establish close coordination with the Department of State, the Central Intelligence Agency, and the National Security Council staff to enhance the ability of the Department of Defense to account for persons covered by subsection (a).
In carrying out the program, the designated Agency Director shall coordinate all external communications and events associated with the program.
